$comment: Space-programme library. Every engineering coefficient the fixture generator and the
engine need lives here rather than as a literal in code, so a site can be re-calibrated
by editing data instead of recompiling. Each entry carries the source it came from.
Heat-balance contract, so nothing is counted twice:
baseHeatLoad  = lightingWPerM2 x area  +  fixedEquipmentW   (always-present gain)
solar         = solarGainMultiplier x solarPeakWPerM2 x area
Equipment power density is deliberately absent from the programmes: it is plugs.go's
job. Putting it in both places is how a cooling load gets double-counted.

calibration.source: Hoang et al. 2021, 57 commercial+government offices, doi:10.55066/proc-icec.2021.19

endUseShares.source: Luong et al. 2025, 45-storey Hanoi tower, doi:10.54772/jomc.v15i02.1190

calibration.gridEmissionFactorSource: Vietnam national grid factor 2022, quoted in Luong et al. 2025

furnishingCapacitanceNote: Room air alone gives a time constant of minutes; real rooms respond over hours
because furniture, partitions and slab surface couple to the air. A lumped 4-6x
multiplier on air capacitance is the standard single-node way to represent that,
and it is what puts identified time constants in the 2-10 h band an office shows.
This is the one number here that is a modelling choice rather than a measurement;
once a room is identified from real data its measured time constant supersedes it.
physics.occupantSensibleSource: ASHRAE Fundamentals, seated light office work, sensible portion
physics.outdoorAirSource: QCVN 09:2017/BXD and ASHRAE 62.1 office ventilation rate
ventilationEnthalpyNote: Total (sensible + latent) enthalpy drop from Ho Chi Minh design outdoor air
(33 C, ~75% RH, ~88 kJ/kg) to the AHU supply condition (~12 C saturated,
~33 kJ/kg). In a tropical climate this fresh-air load is the LARGEST single
cooling term and is mostly latent -- dehumidifying the outdoor air, not cooling
it. Omitting it is why a twin calibrated only on internal gains under-reports a
Vietnamese office by roughly a third. Recomputed live against the weather feed
when outdoor humidity is available; this is the design-day fallback.
physics.designCopNote: Plant COP at nominal lift; simulation/engine.go degrades from this as the building is strained.
physics.eflhNote: Office EFLH for a Vietnamese commercial building on a ~11 h day, 5.5 d week, year-round cooling. Converts a design-day peak to an annual intensity for the cohort check.
physics.supplyAirNote: Design AHU discharge. Superseded per zone by a measured supply-air probe when one reports (edge/esp32 SUPPLY_C).
nonHvacBaseNote: Lighting + fans + lifts + pumps as a floor-area-scaled baseline, replacing a fixed
1.15 MW that was sized for the mis-digitized data-centre fixture and did not move
when the building did. Derived from the case-study shares: outdoor lighting 16.1%
+ ventilation 15.3% + indoor lighting 9.1% + lifts 7.8% + pumps 0.2% = 48.5% of a
109.6 kWh/m2.yr cohort EUI, over ~3000 equivalent full-load hours.
physics.minZoneCapacitanceNote: Numerical floor for the explicit-Euler integration only. The raw digitizer fixture carried 39 kJ/K rooms with 85 kW in them, which ran away; with capacitance derived from real volume this floor should never bind. Raise it if a small zone oscillates.

planning.$comment: Area bands a space planner uses, m2. Drives programme assignment from geometry.
planning.sliverNote: Below this a polygon is a segmentation artifact (wall cavity, column enclosure, duct shaft), not a room. Not conditioned, not credited, not identified.
planning.facadeDepthNote: A zone whose centroid is within one room-depth of the exterior wall sees solar; core zones do not.

open-office.lightingSource: QCVN 09:2017/BXD and ASHRAE 90.1 LED office LPD

comms-room.note: One per floor. A floor comms room is a rack or two on a small split unit. Critical: never swept, never set back.

plant-room.note: AHU / riser plant. Ventilated, not comfort-conditioned. Critical: never swept, never set back.
